body.title: Active inline suppression of false action claims (graduate verifier from detection-only)
body.result: Graduated to active suppression: false action claims stripped from response text. suppress_false_claims() + main.py wiring. 6 new tests.
body.description:
Follow-up to T-igor-emit-action-confabulation (22c57af6). The verifier shipped today is detection-only: it logs CONFAB_CAUGHT and pushes a high-salience TWM marker for the next turn, but does NOT modify response_text. The user still sees the false claim once.

This ticket graduates the verifier to active inline suppression. When a confabulated action claim is detected, intercept the reply at the emit gate and either:

  (a) replace the false claim with 'let me actually do that now' and re-fork the actual action (requires understanding what action was claimed and how to execute it — hardest path)

  (b) suppress the false claim sentence and continue with the rest of the response (medium difficulty — requires sentence boundary detection and confidence that the false claim is local)

  (c) suppress the entire response and fall through to LLM with a 'previous draft contained an unverified action claim' hint (simplest — same shape as PROC_SUPPRESS_MID_CONV_GREETING, fall-through-to-LLM)

DESIGN GATE: observe action_claim_verifier.log for 1-2 weeks post-ship to see what patterns trigger and what replacement strategies would be safe. The detection-only first pass IS the design data collection.

WHEN THE INHIBITORY PATTERN PRIMITIVE LANDS (T-inhibitory-pattern-primitive), this becomes a config change on the existing INHIBITOR_ACTION_CLAIM instance — flip inhibition_action from MARK to REPLACE. No new module.

INERTIA: MEDIUM. Touches reply emit gate.

DEPENDS ON: empirical observation period, T-inhibitory-pattern-primitive (preferred but not strictly required).
